findings.title: Duplicate settled/resolveOnce/rejectOnce pattern across stream functions
findings.description: The settled/resolveOnce/rejectOnce pattern appears identically in both streamFrom (lines 54-66) and streamFromAiSdk (lines 135-137). This is ~10 lines of identical promise-settling logic duplicated across two functions. Both create a Promise, track a boolean 'settled' flag, and define resolveOnce/rejectOnce closures with the exact same semantics.

findings.title: streamFromAiSdk exceeds 50-line threshold at 73 lines
findings.description: streamFromAiSdk is 73 lines long with a maximum nesting depth of 4 levels (function body > Promise constructor > try block > onChunk callback > if/else if chain > inner try block). The function handles three concerns: (1) model/tool setup (lines 122-132), (2) promise settling infrastructure (lines 134-137), and (3) the streaming call with event mapping (lines 139-193). Separating the event-mapping callbacks would reduce both length and nesting.

findings.title: onChunk callback has 4-level nesting with if/else if chain
findings.description: The onChunk handler at nesting level 4 (Promise > try > aiSdkStreamText options > onChunk > try > if/else if) uses a chain of if/else if to dispatch on chunk.type. Each branch is structurally identical: await options?.onStreamEvent?.(mapped event). Using early returns with guard-clause style (if + return) instead of else-if would flatten the logic and make it obvious that each branch is mutually exclusive and self-contained.

findings.title: streamFrom marginally exceeds 50-line threshold at 52 lines
findings.description: streamFrom is 52 lines, just over the 50-line guideline. It is structurally similar to streamFromAiSdk with the same settled/resolveOnce/rejectOnce pattern. If the duplicate settled-promise utility from finding review-simplify-001 is extracted, this function would drop to ~40 lines, resolving this finding automatically. Flagging as P3 since it is only marginally over threshold and would be resolved by addressing the duplication finding.
findings.suggestion:
Extract the settled promise pattern as described in review-simplify-001, which would reduce this function to approximately 40 lines without any other changes needed.

WHY: Marginal over-length would be automatically resolved by the deduplication refactor.

positive_observations: Tool resolution logic was cleanly extracted into resolveXsaiTools, shared by both stream functions
use-ai-sdk.ts composable is well-structured at 64 lines with clear JSDoc, pure adapter pattern, and single responsibility
Guard clause pattern used effectively in createAiSdkModel (early return for anthropic provider)
consciousness.ts change is minimal and clean -- a single reactive ref addition with no complexity increase
The sanitizeMessages function is short, pure, and well-commented
